revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:箸蔵寺]][[zh:箸藏寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Hashikuraji 11.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Omuro school|qid=Q420739|1=Shingon Omuro School}}|prefecture={{ill|Ikedachō Shūzu|qid=Q65272178|1=Ikedachō Shūzu}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.047822222222|N|133.84096111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}|deity={{ill|Kompira|qid=Q167077|1=Konpira Gongen}}|part_of={{ill|Q11419689|qid=Q11419689|1=Q11419689}}; {{ill|Q20044722|qid=Q20044722|1=Q20044722}}}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hashikuraji | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Hasikurazi øa.jpg|250px]] |Caption = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|qid=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Miyoshi, Tokushima|en|Miyoshi, Tokushima|qid=Q990371|lt=Miyoshi City|1=Miyoshi, Tokushima}}, {{ill|Ikedachō Shūzu|ja|池田町州津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikedachō Shūzu|1=Ikedachō Shūzu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65272178}} 1006 Kuradani |coordinates = {{Coord|34|2|52.16|N|133|50|27.46|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Mountain = Hōshūzan |Temple = Shinkōin |Sect = [[Omuro School of Shingon Buddhism]] |Rank = Special Head Temple |deity = {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} |consecration_year = Traditionally [[:en:828 CE|828 CE]] (Tenchō 5) |Founder = Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi) |CulturalProperty = Main Hall, Goma Hall, Abbot's Quarters, Yakushidō, Bell Tower, Tenjin Shrine (National Important Cultural Properties) |Website = [http://www.hashikura.or.jp/ Official Website] }}
'''Hashikuraji''' (箸蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple located in {{ill|Miyoshi, Tokushima|en|Miyoshi, Tokushima|qid=Q990371|lt=Miyoshi City|1=Miyoshi, Tokushima}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|qid=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is officially named ''Hōshūzan Shinkōin Hashikuraji'' and enshrines {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} as its principal deity. The temple is known as the ''Oku-no-in'' (inner sanctuary) of Konpira Shrine in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is part of several pilgrimage routes, including the [[Shikoku Bekkaku Nijūrei]], the [[Shikoku Thirty-six Fudō Shrines]], and others.
== Overview == [[File:Hasikurazi ɨɾ.jpg|180px|thumb|View of the Nio Gate from the Abbot's Quarters]] Hashikuraji is located on the southern slope of {{ill|Hōshūzan|ja|宝珠山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshūzan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (633m above sea level), which is part of the Hashikura Mountain Prefectural Nature Park. The temple's grounds feature a variety of seasonal highlights, such as cherry blossoms and autumn foliage, as well as its role as a major Esoteric Buddhist center.
A ropeway, the [[Hashikura Mountain Ropeway]], connects the mountain's base to the area near the Abbot's Quarters, but visitors must navigate numerous stone steps to reach the temple’s main sites. The ascent to the Main Hall from the Nio Gate involves climbing 769 steps, while the path from the Abbot's Quarters to the Main Hall includes 278 steps inscribed with characters from the ''Heart Sutra'' (般若心経), earning the nickname ''Heart Sutra Stairway.''
Daily Goma rituals (護摩祈祷) are held in the Goma Hall at 6:30 AM and 6:00 PM, continuing an unbroken tradition since the temple's founding. Hashikuraji is also famous for its ''Hashi-kuyō'' (chopstick memorial service) held annually on {{ill|August 4|en|August 4|qid=Q2784|lt=August 4|1=August 4}}.
== History == According to legend, the temple was founded in [[:en:828 CE|828 CE]] (Tenchō 5) during {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}'s pilgrimage through Shikoku. While climbing the mountain, Kūkai encountered a divine manifestation of Konpira Daigongen, who delivered the message: "Those who raise chopsticks in prayer will be saved by my vow." Inspired, Kūkai carved an image of Konpira Daigongen and established a hall to enshrine it.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Hashikuraji was closely associated with the {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|qid=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, serving as its inner sanctuary (''Oku-no-in''). Despite the separation of Shinto and Buddhism (神仏分離令) in the [[Meiji Restoration]], Hashikuraji preserved its syncretic traditions.
== Main Buildings and Grounds == === Nio Gate Area === * '''Nio Gate (仁王門)''': The main entrance to the temple grounds, with a parking area nearby. * '''Takadōrō (高灯籠)''': A tall stone lantern that once served as a landmark for boats navigating the Yoshino River. * '''Kuratani Training Grounds (蔵谷の行場)''': A sacred site for ascetic practices located in the valley below the gate.
=== Abbot's Quarters Area === * '''Abbot's Quarters (方丈)''': Houses a statue of Kōbō Daishi and serves as the administrative center of the temple. * '''Goma Hall (護摩殿)''': A three-part structure housing a Goma altar and the main image of Konpira Daigongen. * '''Bell Tower (鐘楼堂)''': A striking architectural feature. * '''Heart Sutra Stairway (般若心経昇経段)''': A staircase of 278 steps inscribed with characters from the ''Heart Sutra.''
=== Main Hall Area === * '''Main Hall (本殿)''': Enshrines the temple's secret principal image, Konpira Daigongen, along with attendant deities. * '''Kannon Hall (観音堂)''': A small, secluded hall housing Batō Kannon. * '''Daishi Hall (大師堂)''': Features a statue of Kōbō Daishi and serves as a focus for pilgrimages. * '''Mini Shikoku Pilgrimage (ミニ四国八十八ヶ所)''': A miniature replica of the Shikoku pilgrimage.
== Cultural Properties == ; '''National Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Hall * Goma Hall * Bell Tower * Abbot's Quarters * Yakushi Hall * Tenjin Shrine
; '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties''' * Nio Gate * Takadōrō * Middle Gate * Chōzusha (purification basin)
; '''Tokushima Prefectural Cultural Property''' * Kannon Hall
== Access == ; By Train * JR {{ill|Dosan Line|en|Dosan Line|qid=Q861077|lt=Dosan Line|1=Dosan Line}}: {{ill|Hashikura Station|en|Hashikura Station|qid=Q900476|lt=Hashikura Station|1=Hashikura Station}} (0.5km from the temple) ; By Car * {{ill|Tokushima Expressway|en|Tokushima Expressway|qid=Q11490216|lt=Tokushima Expressway|1=Tokushima Expressway}}: Exit at [[Ikawa Ikeda IC]], then take National Route 32 to the Hashikura Mountain Ropeway.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.hashikura.or.jp/ Official Website] * [http://wwwd.pikara.ne.jp/hashikurasan/ Hashikura Mountain Ropeway Official Site] * [https://www.bekkaku.com/?page_id=43 Shikoku Bekkaku 20 Temples - Hashikuraji]
{{translated page|ja|箸蔵寺|version=103782664|comment=Imported full ja history}}
